finding_results.notes: Tomas now bargains in county-work specifics: falsified gallons, spray-line cover stories, decimal scrutiny, key loss, overtime loss, and misused county gas. Mina answers from the spoonbill-creek obsession and from the bodily shame of wanting Tomas as part of the same ugly bargain.
finding_results.summary: The dock bargaining stretch now separates their voices and leverage: Tomas sounds like an overworked county airboat pilot guarding his job and fuel log, while Mina stays fixed on the spoonbill creek, the blue rumor, and the compromising fact that she wants him too.

summary: Revised the chapter_02 bargaining scene to resolve the dialogue/idiolect/cadence finding while preserving the chapter's transactional turn into illegal night access.
